properties.remark: HIGHWAY 66 AND TYLER RD ARE WASHED OUT IN SEVERAL LOCATIONS WITH TREES DOWN.
RELAYED VIA KOIN. WATER SPOUT REMAINED OFFSHORE. WATER SPOUT DURATION 5 MINUTES.
LARGE HAIL, UNKNOWN SIZE. POWER LINE STRUCK BY LIGHTNING AND KNOCKED OUT PHONE LINES

A SPOTTER IN DAY CALIFORNIA REPORTED A MUDSLIDE UP AGAINST HER HOUSE. THIS MUDSLIDE WAS ASSOCIATED WITH A BURN SCARRED AREA AND TWO HOUSES WERE AFFECTED. A SHERIFF DEPUTY REPORTED THAT CLOGGED CULVERTS CAUSED MUD AND WATER TO OVERFLOW THE ROAD.
HEAVY RAINFALL WITH FLOODING OF NEARBY ROADS. HAIL ALSO STRIPPING LEAVES FROM TREES. OBSERVER LOCATION 1.5 MI W DOWNTOWN WILLIAMS.

A LOCAL FIRE INFORMATION OFFICER REPORTED THAT A GUST FRONT FROM A COLLAPSING THUNDERSTORM CAME THROUGH THE AREA FROM TAKILMA ROAD TO HOLLAND LOOP AROUND 5 PM. HE ESTIMATED WINDS WERE 30-40 MPH. THE WINDS UPROOTED SEVERAL TREES, WHICH IN TURN TOOK OUT POWERLINES AND STARTED FIRES. AS OF 900 PM...ALL FIRES WERE UNDER CONTROL. THE IMET ON THE BUCKSKIN FIRE,REPORTED MINOR ROOF AND SIDEWALL DAMAGE TO THE ROUGH AND READY MILLL NEAR CAVE JUNCTION. THE STRONGEST WINDS RECORDED NEAR THE AREA WAS A GUST TO 28 MPH AT THE ILLINOIS VALLEY AIRPORT RAWS.

THE BUCKSKIN FIRE INFORMATION OFFICER REPORTED THAT A GUST FRONT FROM A COLLAPSING THUNDERSTORM CAME THROUGH THE AREA FROM TAKILMA ROAD TO HOLLAND LOOP AROUND 5 PM. HE ESTIMATED WINDS WERE 30-40 MPH. THE WINDS UPROOTED SEVERAL TREES, WHICH IN TURN TOOK OUT POWERLINES AND STARTED FIRES. AS OF 900 PM...ALL FIRES WERE UNDER CONTROL. JON BONK, THE IMET ON THE BUCKSKIN FIRE, REPORTED MINOR ROOF AND SIDEWALL DAMAGE TO THE ROUGH AND READY MILLL NEAR CAVE JUNCTION. THE STRONGEST WINDS RECORDED NEAR THE AREA WAS A GUST TO 28 MPH AT THE ILLINOIS VALLEY AIRPORT RAWS.
REPORT OF ESTIMATED GOLF BALL SIZED HAIL WHILE DRIVING ON HIGHWAY 3 BETWEEN FORT JONES AND ETNA. BASED ON OTHER REPORTS AND WITHOUT A FIRSTHAND SOURCE, USING QUARTER SIZED AS THE OFFICIAL.
GENERAL STORE EMPLOYEE REPORTS GOLF BALL SIZED HAIL WITH THUNDERSTORM.
BROADCAST MET REPORTED GUSTS OF 50-60MPH IN ASHLAND
WIND GUSTS KNOCKED DOWN POWER LINES
TREES DOWN AT CHURCH OF JESUS CHRIST OF LATTER-DAY SAINTS
NUMEROUS REPORTS THROUGHOUT MEDFORD OF TREES AND POWER LINES DOWN. ALSO INVESTIGATING A FEW REPORTS OF FIRES DUE TO LIGHTNING.
YREKA DISPATCH REPORTED FLOODING OF HOMES IN GRENADA AND EVACUATIONS OCCURRING AT SHEPARD OF GOD ASSISTED LIVING CENTER IN GRENADA DUE TO WATER ENTERING THE BUILDING.
SISKIYOU COUNTY SHERIFFS DEPARTMENT REPORTS FLASH FLOODING ON THE MUD CREEK BASIN.
SISKIYOU COUNTY SHERIFFS DEPARTMENT REPORTS FLASH FLOODING IN THE CITY OF MCCLOUD DUE TO RUNOFF FROM HEAVY RAINFALL.

SPOTTER REPORTED VISIBILITY DOWN TO 500 YARDS AT TIMES. MOTORISTS ON PORTIONS OF HIGHWAY 31 HAD TO REDUCE SPEED TO 10 MPH. TECHNICALLY, THIS IS A BLOWING DUST REPORT. OBSERVER LOCATION MP 72 ON HWY 31.
